title: Sum of Distances in Cactus
description: Find the sum of the distances between all pairs of vertices in a cactus graph. A cactus graph is a graph in which every edge belongs to at most one simple cycle. The distance between vertices is calculated as the number of edges in the shortest path connecting a given pair of vertices.
input:
First line contains two integers $n$ and $m$ --- the number of vertices and the number of edges in the cactus. 

Each of the following $m$ lines contains two integers $u_i$ $v_i$ --- the numeric labels of vertices connected by an edge. 

It is guaranteed that the graph is connected and does not have self-loops and multiple edges.
output: Output a single line containing the sum of the distances between all pairs of vertices.
source: Contest, Open Cup, 2018/2019 Season, Stage 18: Grand Prix of Minsk
